detail: Next.js 14.2+ App Router with React Server Components, TypeScript strict mode, shadcn/ui component library (New York style), SQLite via better-sqlite3 with WAL mode for concurrent reads, fintech dark theme (slate-900 bg + emerald/amber accents), tsx for script execution, CSS variables for theming, lucide-react for icons. Tax bracket data hardcoded from authoritative IRS/CRA sources (Rev. Proc. 2024-40, CRA 2025 tables) with JSDoc references. Progressive tax calculation using marginal bracket system. All three tasks are independent and can be executed in parallel.

Transform TaxBridge MVP into a revenue-generating SaaS product targeting 50K+ H-1B/TN visa tech workers at FAANG companies. Phase 1: Launch freemium model with Stripe payments and SEO-optimized landing pages. Phase 2: Build enterprise features for immigration law firms managing 100+ clients. Revenue target: $83K/month via $299/year individual subscriptions (280 users) + $2K/year enterprise seats (25 firms).

Sprint 0 complete. All 14 tasks done. Starting next sprint.

Using jsPDF over alternatives (pdfmake, react-pdf) for simpler server-side generation without React component complexity. Recharts for FTC visualization provides responsive charts with minimal bundle size. All calculations server-side in API routes for security (no client-side tax logic exposure). SQLite queries via better-sqlite3 synchronous API for simplicity in Next.js route handlers. Treaty Article XV logic centralized in ftc-calculator.ts for reusability across UI and PDF export.

Next.js 15 App Router with Server Components for data fetching, better-sqlite3 for local database (already in use), Radix UI for accessible accordion component, Bank of Canada Valet API for live exchange rates with 24-hour caching strategy, TailwindCSS for styling (existing), TypeScript for type safety (existing). Database schema extends existing tables (users, rsu_events, tax_calculations) with exchange_rates and form_completion. MVP uses hardcoded user_id=1 to avoid auth complexity.

**Stack:** Next.js 15 App Router, TypeScript, TailwindCSS, shadcn/ui (Radix UI primitives), better-sqlite3 (SQLite DB already initialized at `/lib/db/init.ts`), Zod validation (already in use), react-hook-form, Vitest for testing. **Architecture:** Server-side API routes for data fetching, server components for pages, client components for interactive forms. **Tax Logic:** Article XV treaty proration for US-sourced income, FTC calculation to prevent double taxation. **Exchange Rates:** Bank of Canada API via `/lib/currency.ts` or hardcoded 1.35 CAD/USD for MVP. **UI:** Modern fintech aesthetic with gradient accents (blue-600 to indigo-600), shadcn/ui components, responsive 2-column layout, color-coded tax brackets, animated progress bars.

Next.js 14+ App Router for modern React architecture with server components, better-sqlite3 for zero-config embedded database (no network overhead, ideal for MVP), shadcn/ui for production-ready accessible components, TypeScript strict mode for type safety on financial calculations, TailwindCSS for rapid fintech-grade UI development with dark mode support.

Build TaxBridge MVP in 4 phases: (1) establish Next.js foundation with SQLite database and TypeScript types, (2) implement core RSU entry and dual-country tax calculation engine using 2024/2025 tax brackets and treaty Article XV, (3) build user-facing dashboard with currency conversion and forms checklist, (4) add Foreign Tax Credit optimizer to eliminate double taxation. Prioritize working end-to-end flow over polish.
